subject: Geography
title: The Borderless Digital World
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Borderless Digital World.

hook.text: The internet was designed without borders, but governments are building digital walls.

buildup.text: China's Great Firewall blocks Google, Facebook, and most Western platforms.

discovery.text: Russia, Iran, and North Korea also filter what their citizens can see online.

twist.text: Even democracies use 'geo-blocking'—content available in one country is blocked in another.

climax.text: The dream of a borderless internet is fading as nations rebuild walls in cyberspace.

punchline.text: The internet has no borders. Governments disagree.

quiz.question: What is China's Great Firewall?
options: A system that blocks access to many Western websites
A physical wall protecting data centers
